c74object.name: voice-alligator-audio~
c74object.digest: audio-to-audio voice allocator with built-in ADSR and glide
c74object.description:
voice-alligator-audio~ is the audio-input companion to voice-alligator~. Rather than receiving MIDI-style messages, it is driven entirely by signals: a trigger impulse, a pitch signal, and a velocity signal.
Notes are triggered by a rising edge (i.e. a click~) on inlet 1. Pitch and velocity are sampled at the moment of the trigger on inlets 2 and 3 respectively.
Most parameters (pitch, velocity, ADSR, glide, mono) are set via list messages (i.e. attack 1000 500).
By default, all parameter lists have one entry. The current settings can be printed to the console using a print message or double-clicking the object.
voice-alligator-audio~ can sample-accurately trigger 2 or more voices simultaneously with a single impulse, with per-voice pitch, velocity, ADSR envelope, and glide settings. The longest of the parameter lists will decide how many voices are started on a single trigger to inlet 1.
If a non-zero signal is present on inlets 2 or 3 at the time of the trigger, it overrides the corresponding pitch / vel list values.
Each voice is allocated to a stream position. For example, if the longest list has 3 entries, up to 3 voices can be triggered simultaneously: one for stream 1 (list index 1), one for stream 2 (list index 2), and one for stream 3 (list index 3). When a list has fewer entries than the longest list, values wrap around with modulo indexing.
So for example if we initialize the object and send it a message attack 1000 500 300, vel 0.5 0.2, on the next impulse it will trigger 3 notes.
Note 1: attack 1000ms and vel 0.5
Note 2: attack 500ms and vel 0.2
Note 3: attack 300ms and vel 0.5
The envelope is a one-shot ADSR: attack, decay, sustain hold (sustain_dur), then release. The total ramp length equals attack + decay + sustain_dur + release.
c74object.discussion:
Inlet 1 — trigger signal
A rising edge (transition from 0 to any non-zero value) fires note-on(s) for all streams.
Inlet 2 — pitch signal
When non-zero at the moment of a trigger, overrides all pitch list values. When scale is off (default): a raw frequency in Hz. When scale is on: a MIDI note number looked up in the internal scale array.
Inlet 3 — velocity signal
When non-zero at the moment of a trigger, overrides all vel list values. Normalised to 0–1.
Inlet 4 — external busy (MC signal)
An MC signal with one channel per voice. While a channel is non-zero, the corresponding voice is kept busy. A 1→0 transition on channel v ends voice v: the ADSR is released with a declick ramp (duration controlled by declick_ms), after which the voice is freed normally.
Use this to keep a voice busy for as long as an external process (e.g. a one-shot sample player) is running. Route the busy signal back here via a one-vector delay to allow feedback. When unconnected this inlet receives a constant 0 and has no effect.
MC Signal Outlets
Outlet 1 — freq~
Frequency in Hz.
Outlet 2 — env~
ADSR envelope output, velocity-scaled. Peak equals the normalised velocity.
Outlet 3 — impulse~
A single sample impulse fired at the start of each non-glide note attack. Set glide_impulse to also fire on glide notes.
Outlet 4 — ramp~
A linear ramp from 0.0 at note-on to 1.0 at the end of the release phase. Total ramp length equals attack + decay + sustain_dur + release. Outputs 0.0 when the voice is inactive.
Outlet 5 — stream~
Stream index of each voice (1-indexed). Indicates which stream position triggered this voice. Outputs 0 when the voice is inactive.
Stream-based voice allocation
Each list position corresponds to a stream. On each trigger, each stream either allocates a new voice or — if mono is enabled for that stream — glides the existing voice to the new pitch.
ADSR parameter changes
ADSR parameter changes take effect on the next note-on only. Currently playing voices are not affected.

method.name: pitch
method.digest: Set pitch list (Hz or MIDI note numbers)
method.description:
A list of frequencies (Hz) or MIDI note numbers (when scale is on). Each entry corresponds to a stream position. The number of notes triggered per rising edge equals the length of the longest parameter list (minimum 1).
default: 440.0 Hz

method.name: vel
method.description:
A list of velocities normalised to 0–1.
default: 0.5

method.name: mono
method.description:
A list of 0/1 flags controlling per-stream monophonic glide behaviour. When mono is enabled for a stream, a retrigger glides the existing voice to the new pitch instead of allocating a fresh voice. When mono is off (default), every trigger allocates a new voice.
Example: mono 1 0 0 — stream 1 glides, streams 2 and 3 always allocate new voices.
default: 0 (off) for all streams

method.name: mono_steals_release
method.description:
A list of 0/1 flags. When mono is on and a voice on the same stream is in its release phase, this flag determines whether the new note glides the releasing voice (1) or allocates a fresh voice (0).
Only relevant when mono is enabled for the stream.
default: 0 (off) — releasing voices are not glided

method.name: active
method.description:
When off, incoming triggers are ignored. Already-playing voices are unaffected.
default: 1 (on)

method.name: attack
method.description:
Per-stream attack times in milliseconds.
default: 10 ms

method.name: decay
method.description:
Per-stream decay times in milliseconds.
default: 100 ms

method.name: sustain
method.description:
Per-stream sustain levels as a fraction of peak (0–1). The actual sustain amplitude is sustain level x velocity.
default: 0.8

method.name: sustain_dur
method.description:
Per-stream sustain hold time in milliseconds. After the decay phase reaches the sustain level, the envelope holds for this duration before entering the release phase.
default: 100 ms

method.name: release
method.description:
Per-stream release times in milliseconds.
default: 300 ms

method.name: attack_curve
method.description:
Per-stream attack curve shape. 0 is linear, positive values are convex (fast start), negative values are concave (slow start).
default: 0

method.name: decay_curve
method.description:
Per-stream decay curve shape. Same convention as attack_curve.
default: 0

method.name: release_curve
method.description:
Per-stream release curve shape. Same convention as attack_curve.
default: 0

method.name: glidetime
method.description:
Per-stream glide time in milliseconds.
default: 30 ms

method.name: glide_curve
method.description:
Per-stream glide curve shape. Same convention as attack_curve.
default: 0

method.name: glide_retrigger
method.description:
Per-stream flag. 0 (legato): envelope runs uninterrupted through the glide. 1 (retrigger): envelope restarts from current level with new velocity.
default: 0 (legato)

method.name: glide_impulse
method.description:
Per-stream flag. When on, outlet 3 fires a 1-sample impulse on glide notes as well as fresh note-ons.
default: 0 (off)

method.name: debug
method.description:
Prints trigger, voice allocation, and ADSR completion events to the Max console.
default: 0 (off)

method.name: scale_def
method.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. Only relevant when scale is on.
See the voice-alligator reference for full documentation.

method.name: reset
method.description: Clears all parameter lists (pitch, vel, mono, attack, decay, sustain, sustain_dur, release, attack_curve, decay_curve, release_curve, glidetime, glide_curve, glide_retrigger, glide_impulse) and restores default values.

method.name: print
method.description: Prints the current value(s) of every parameter to the Max console.

attribute.name: steal_mode
attribute.digest: Voice stealing mode: 0 oldest, 1 most advanced (default oldest)
attribute.description:
Steal mode determines which voice is stolen when a new trigger arrives and no free voice is available.
0 (oldest): steal the voice that has been active the longest.
1 (most advanced): steal the voice that is furthest along in its ADSR envelope.
default: 0 (oldest)

attribute.name: scale
attribute.digest: Use scale array for pitch lookup (default false)
attribute.description:
When off (default), pitch values and inlet 2 are treated as raw frequencies in Hz.
When on, values are rounded to the nearest integer and used as MIDI note numbers to look up a frequency in the internal scale array. Use scale_def (see voice-alligator reference) to define the tuning.
default: false

attribute.name: declick_ms
attribute.description:
When a voice is stolen, a declick ramp of this duration is applied before the new attack begins.
default: 5 ms

attribute.name: scale_def_mode
attribute.digest: Scale definition mode: 0 MIDI, 1 frequency (default frequency)
attribute.description:
Determines how values in a scale_def message are interpreted.
default: 1 (frequency)

attribute.name: scale_fill
attribute.digest: Fill undefined scale entries with MTOF values (default true)
